title: Redirect Semantics, Method Rewriting, Credential Scope, and Loop Control · Automation and Data Client Systems · Program Forensics
summary: A data reliability engineer tracing a failed overnight import must isolate a plausible but incorrect result from conflicting traces, repair its cause, and retain a regression witness for redirect semantics, method rewriting, credential scope, and loop control, producing auditable redirect policy engine. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers auditable redirect policy engine and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record.…
objectives: Differentiate 301, 302, 303, 307, and 308 cache, permanence, method, and body consequences. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Resolve Location references against the current request URL and validate the resulting destination each hop. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Bound redirect hops, detect loops, retain the chain, and expose the policy decision to callers. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Redirect Status Semantics
steps.instruction:
debug case — overnight ingestion repair client sends POST P-4 with authorization, then receives 302 ../moved, 307 https://upload.example.test/next, and a loop back to the first URL. Predict method, body, credential, origin, hop-budget, and SSRF decisions per step. Competency probe: Differentiate 301, 302, 303, 307, and 308 cache, permanence, method, and body consequences. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Inspect the Redirect Status Semantics trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

At Python Publish the incident record · Debug Manual Redirect Defense, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: overnight ingestion repair client sends POST P-2 with authorization, then receives 302 ../moved, 307 https://upload.example.test/next, and a loop back to the first URL. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

For Python Publish the incident record · Debug Manual Redirect Defense, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Following redirects is transport plumbing with no application or security semantics. This remains false for Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers.
